title: Malta proposes DeFi rulebook covering DAOs under MiCA-era framework
summary: The Maltese regulator is seeking industry feedback on a legal framework for software-governed organizations, arguing that many DeFi projects are not fully decentralized.

title: Bitcoin decouples from tech stocks: Is $60K BTC’s next stop?
summary: Bitcoin’s slump accelerated as capital rotated further into the AI sector, raising the odds of a BTC price drop below $60,000.

title: Gold Set for Weekly Loss as Hawkish Fed Outweighs Peace Deal
summary: Gold was on track for a third weekly loss, as a hawkish Federal Reserve and rate-hike bets outweighed the signing of an interim peace deal between the US and Iran.

title: Oil Set for Deep Weekly Loss as Hormuz Traffic Starts to Inch Up
summary: Oil headed for a substantial weekly drop as the US-Iran interim peace deal saw shipping through the Strait of Hormuz start to return to normal, easing the global crude market’s biggest ever supply shock.

title: China’s Z.AI Releases GLM-5.2: A Model That Rivals Claude Opus—Using Zero Nvidia Chips
summary: Z.ai's GLM-5.2 sits within 1% of Claude Opus 4.8 on long-horizon coding benchmarks, runs entirely on Huawei silicon, and undercuts Western frontier models by up to 82% per token.

title: Midjourney Pivots From AI Images to Medical Imaging, Aiming to Build a Better MRI Alternative
summary: Image generation company Midjourney is developing a full-body imaging system that combines ultrasound tech with AI smarts.

title: Strategy’s preferred stock STRC continues to trade under $90 as trading volume jumps
summary: Strategy's preferred STRC stock closed again under $90 for the second consecutive day at $88.59, having drifted as low as $82.50.

title: CryptoQuant says bitcoin microtransactions now make up 80% of daily transactions
summary: Bitcoin transactions below 0.01 BTC now make up about 80% of all daily transactions, up from about 44% in 2023, CryptoQuant said.

title: JD Vance lashes out at Israeli government over Iran deal criticism
summary: US vice-president says Israelis who find fault in agreement with Tehran need ‘to wake up and smell the reality’

title: US-Iran accord opens way for Hormuz charges, industry warns
summary: Shipping executives fear language will enable Tehran to introduce fees after 60 days or a fund similar to Strait of Malacca
